---
applyTo: "README.md,install.md,compatibility.md,AGENTS.md,.github/copilot-instructions.md,.github/instructions/**/*.instructions.md,.github/skills/**/*.md,docs/jacks/**/*.md"
---

Use `docs/jacks/` as the longer reference set when a repo-facing file needs more detail.
Write each file for its actual reader.
Keep the public voice modest, plain, and practical.
Do not use hype language or drag in outside product names.
Use `repo-scoped`, `user-scoped`, `cross-project`, and `environment-specific` precisely.
Do not present `docs/jacks/inventory.json`, `docs/jacks/hook-contracts/`, or workflow documents as runtime features beyond what GitHub docs currently describe.
Keep `.github/skills/` and `.github/hooks/` honest, documented, and narrowly described.
If a policy already has a clear home, update that home instead of retelling it elsewhere.
